← All worlds/World 10
World 10 · 1 state

Spiritual Awakening

Films for the moment the small self steps aside — and something older, wider, and somehow more familiar walks through.

Choose an emotion
Beyond Ego
On Beyond Ego

Something shifted. You cannot explain it cleanly to anyone, and you have stopped trying. The self you used to perform — the one with a career arc and an opinion on everything and a very specific idea of what success looked like — feels less like who you are and more like a role you were assigned. This is disorienting. It is also, quietly, a kind of relief. The films here understand the sensation of outgrowing the container. They were made by people who noticed the same thing and turned it into something you can sit with for two hours and come out the other side changed.

Beyond Ego

Films for the dissolution that turned out to be the door.

Nostalghia

Andrei Tarkovsky · 1983

CS8.6

Baraka

Ron Fricke · 1992

CS9.2
1999

The Matrix

The Wachowskis

View Film →

A hacker discovers the world he knows is a simulation and is offered the chance to wake up into the truth. Cinema’s definitive metaphor about waking up.

CS8.6
2012

Life of Pi

Ang Lee

View Film →

A young Indian boy survives a shipwreck adrift in a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger — and tells two versions of his story decades later.

CS8.5
2011

Samsara

Ron Fricke

View Film →

A non-narrative documentary filmed across twenty-five countries on five continents — ritual, industry, beauty, and decay, with no dialogue.

CS8.9
2011

The Tree of Life

Terrence Malick

View Film →

A Texas family in the 1950s and their oldest son’s adult life, intercut with the formation of the universe itself.

CS8.7
2003

Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter… and Spring

Kim Ki-duk

View Film →

A young monk’s life unfolds across five seasons in a tiny Buddhist temple that floats on a remote Korean lake.

CS9.0
2020

Soul

Pete Docter

View Film →

A middle-school music teacher who finally lands the gig of his dreams dies on the same day — and finds himself in a celestial waystation, trying to get back.

CS7.9
2006

The Fountain

Darren Aronofsky

View Film →

A man across three timelines — conquistador, modern scientist, and space-traveller — tries to save the woman he loves from death.

CS8.6
1992

Baraka

Ron Fricke

View Film →

A non-narrative documentary travelling across twenty-four countries, capturing ritual, prayer, work, and landscape — a wordless prayer at planetary scale.

CS9.1
2016

Arrival

Denis Villeneuve

View Film →

A linguist is recruited to help communicate with newly-arrived alien visitors — and discovers that learning their language begins to bend her relationship with time.

CS9.1
1998

The Truman Show

Peter Weir

View Film →

A man who has unknowingly lived his entire life inside a televised, scripted reality slowly begins to suspect the truth and look for the door.

CS9.7
1968

2001: A Space Odyssey

Stanley Kubrick

View Film →

From ape to astronaut to stargate. A film about the evolution of consciousness and the thing that might be watching it happen.

CS9.4
1966

Andrei Rublev

Andrei Tarkovsky

View Film →

Episodes from the life of a medieval icon painter who eventually rediscovers his faith through witnessing human suffering.

CS9.0
1983

Nostalghia

Andrei Tarkovsky

View Film →

A Russian poet in Italy searching for a lost composer becomes attached to a local madman’s belief that the world can be saved by carrying a candle across a drained pool.

CS8.3
1997

Kundun

Martin Scorsese

View Film →

The life of the fourteenth Dalai Lama from his discovery as a child to his exile from Tibet in 1959.

CS8.6
1986

The Mission

Roland Joffé

View Film →

A Spanish Jesuit and a reformed slave trader protect a mission community in 18th-century South America from colonial forces.

CS9.0
1963

Winter Light

Ingmar Bergman

View Film →

A rural Swedish pastor loses his faith on the day a fisherman comes to him for help with existential dread.

CS8.6
1999

The Green Mile

Frank Darabont

View Film →

A death row officer on a Southern US prison block meets an inmate with mysterious healing powers in 1935.

CS8.5
1997

Contact

Robert Zemeckis

View Film →

A scientist receives a signal from deep space and builds a machine to travel to its source. The journey is not what she expected.

CS8.8
2016

Silence

Martin Scorsese

View Film →

Two Jesuit priests enter 17th-century Japan to find their mentor. The silence of God in the face of suffering is the film’s subject.

CS8.0
2014

I Origins

Mike Cahill

View Film →

A molecular biologist studying the eye encounters evidence that challenges his materialist view of consciousness.

CS7.9
2012

Cloud Atlas

Wachowskis & Tykwer

View Film →

Six interconnected stories across six centuries, suggesting the same soul making the same choices across lifetimes.

CS8.7
2015

Embrace of the Serpent

Ciro Guerra

View Film →

Two Amazonian expeditions decades apart seek a sacred healing plant. Told in black-and-white.

CS9.0
1983

Nostalghia

Andrei Tarkovsky

View Film →

A Russian poet in Italy becomes attached to a local madman who believes carrying a candle across a drained thermal pool can save the world.

CS8.6
1992

Baraka

Ron Fricke

View Film →

Non-narrative documentary across twenty-four countries: ritual, prayer, industry, landscape, and the variety of human life on a single planet.

Hope →

For the first line of light under the door.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
